items.check: Confirm the Button receives focus in the expected sequential tab order relative to surrounding content
items.example: Tab stop 1: Button receives focus before any subsequent interactive elements. Focus order matches visual reading order (left to right, top to bottom).

items.check: Verify reading order of button label and any supplementary icon or badge follows logical DOM sequence
items.example: Reading order: [Icon (decorative, hidden from AT)] → [Button label text]. Icon does not interrupt label announcement.

items.check: If button appears inside a form, verify it is correctly typed as submit, reset, or button to avoid unintended form submission
items.example: Button type='submit' when used as a primary form action. Button type='button' when used for non-submission actions such as opening a modal.

items.check: Confirm any required field hints or validation errors associated with the button action are programmatically linked and visible
items.example: If the button triggers form validation, ensure error summary is announced via aria-live or focus is moved to the error message.

items.check: Every button variant must have a meaningful accessible name that describes its action
items.example: Accessible name: 'Submit form'. Do not use generic labels like 'Click here' or 'Button'. If button is icon-only, provide aria-label='Close dialog'.

items.check: Annotate all interactive states: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, and loading
items.example: States: Default | Hover (background changes to brand-dark) | Focus (2px solid #0057B8 offset 2px) | Active (pressed, scale 0.97) | Disabled (aria-disabled='true', opacity 40%, not focusable) | Loading (aria-busy='true', aria-label='Loading, please wait').

items.check: Disabled button state must be communicated to assistive technology without relying solely on color
items.example: Disabled: Use aria-disabled='true' rather than the HTML disabled attribute when button should remain in focus order. Visual indicator: muted gray fill + 'not-allowed' cursor. Do not rely on color alone.

items.check: Icon-only button variants must include a visible or programmatic text alternative
items.example: Icon-only button: Add aria-label='Delete item' or pair with visually-hidden <span> containing button purpose text. Tooltip alone is insufficient.

items.check: Button with icon and text: confirm icon is marked decorative so it is not double-announced
items.example: Icon inside button: aria-hidden='true' on the element. Screen reader announces only the visible text label.

items.check: Verify button role is correctly identified — native <button> element preferred; if using a <div> or <a>, role='button' must be explicitly set
items.example: Use native <button> element. If a custom element is required, annotate: role='button', tabindex='0', keyboard: Enter and Space activate the button.

items.check: Confirm button label does not change unexpectedly between states in a way that loses context
items.example: Toggle button: aria-pressed='false' (label: 'Mute') → aria-pressed='true' (label: 'Unmute'). Or keep label static 'Mute' and rely on aria-pressed change.

items.check: Loading state on button must announce status change to screen reader users via live region
items.example: When button enters loading state: inject aria-live='polite' region with text 'Loading, please wait.' or use aria-busy='true' on the button with aria-label updated to 'Saving, please wait'.

items.check: Success or error feedback triggered by a button action must be surfaced to assistive technology
items.example: After form submission button is activated: announce result via aria-live='assertive' region — e.g., 'Form submitted successfully' or 'Error: Please correct 2 fields before submitting'.

items.check: Button must be activatable with both Enter and Space keys
items.example: Enter: activates button action. Space: activates button action (especially important for custom role='button' elements). Prevent default scroll on Space keypress.

items.check: Focus indicator must be visible and meet minimum contrast ratio on all button variants
items.example: Focus ring: 2px solid outline, color #0057B8, offset 2px from button edge. Contrast ratio of focus indicator against adjacent color must be at least 3:1 (WCAG 2.2 criterion 2.4.11).

items.check: No keyboard trap — user must be able to Tab away from the button without restriction
items.example: Tab: moves focus to next interactive element. Shift+Tab: moves focus to previous interactive element. No custom key handler prevents standard navigation.

items.check: If button opens a disclosure, modal, or dropdown, annotate the expected keyboard interaction for that pattern
items.example: Button opens modal: Enter/Space opens modal, focus moves to first focusable element inside modal. Escape closes modal and returns focus to the triggering button.

items.check: Document any keyboard shortcuts defined in the Keyboard Shortcuts instance within Button Details
items.example: Custom shortcut: Alt+S submits the primary action. Annotate: this shortcut must not conflict with browser or OS reserved shortcuts (WCAG 2.1.4 — single character shortcuts must be remappable or disableable).

items.check: Button touch target size must meet minimum dimensions to support users with motor impairments
items.example: Minimum touch target: 44×44px (WCAG 2.5.5 AAA) or at minimum 24×24px with sufficient spacing (WCAG 2.5.8 AA). Annotate intended tap target area on small/compact button variants.

items.check: Button activation should use the up-event (pointer up / mouseup) not the down-event to allow users to cancel accidental activations
items.example: Activate button action on pointerup/mouseup, not pointerdown/mousedown. This allows users to drag pointer away to cancel the action before releasing.

items.check: Hover state tooltip or additional info shown on hover must also be accessible on focus for keyboard users
items.example: If a tooltip or contextual hint appears on mouse hover, it must also appear on keyboard focus. Tooltip must be dismissible with Escape key and persistent enough to be read.

items.check: Review the 'Button Details' component body for completeness — element, accessible name, description, and type fields should all be filled in for each variant
items.example: Note: Confirm 'element' field specifies <button> (preferred) or custom role. 'type' field should specify submit / button / reset per use case. 'Accessible name' field should cover all naming strategies used across variants.

items.check: Confirm the VRT (Visual Regression Testing) frames include all interactive states so regressions in focus or disabled styling are caught
items.example: VRT note: Ensure VRT test cases cover Default, Hover, Focus, Active, Disabled, and Loading states for all size and style variants. Missing state coverage may allow accessibility regressions to go undetected.

items.check: Confirm color contrast ratio for button label text against button background meets WCAG AA minimums across all variants
items.example: Primary button: white label on brand blue — ratio must be ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text, ≥ 3:1 for large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold). Verify ghost/outline button contrast on both light and dark backgrounds.

items.check: Review 'How to: Button' documentation frame to verify accessibility guidance is included alongside usage guidance for consuming teams
items.example: How-to note: Add accessibility section covering: accessible name requirements, state management, icon-only usage rules, and when to use aria-describedby for supplementary context.

items.check: Verify Pin Stamp, Bracket Stamp, and Lasso Stamp components are correctly linking each button variant to its corresponding Button Details annotation
items.example: Canvas utility check: Each stamp instance should be paired with a 'Button Details' callout via 'Pair Stamp with Details'. Confirm no orphaned stamps exist without a linked details panel.

items.check: Confirm Lasso Stamp label positions are consistent across similar annotation groupings for visual clarity of the annotation layer
items.example: Canvas utility note: Standardize lasso label position to 'bottom-right ↓↘' or 'right →' for button group annotations to avoid label overlap with other annotation layers.

items.check: Confirm the 'Details VRT' and 'Lasso VRT' frames in the VRT tests section include coverage for annotation stamps themselves to detect annotation breakage on library update
items.example: VRT canvas note: Lasso VRT and Details VRT frames should cover at minimum one instance of each stamp variant (Pin, Bracket, Lasso) paired with a Button Details panel to catch stamp layout regressions.
